Pancharama Kshetras

[Telugu History](https://sadashree.substack.com/s/telugu-history/?utm_source=substack&utm_medium=menu)

# Pancharama Kshetras

[](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

![Ratnakar Sadasyula's avatar](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!R73I!,w_36,h_36,c_fill,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F81a2c542-8b3f-4ade-98d2-a40950cffbe3_2640x1980.jpeg)

[Ratnakar Sadasyula](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

Feb 05, 2026

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

1

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

1

Share

Pancharama Kshetras are called so, after the five temples dedicated to Shiva, in coastal Andhra. All Shiva linga at the five temples were apparently, derived from a single linga, possesed by the Asura king Tarakasura which granted him near immortality and made him invincible, as long as he wore it.

The Devatas requested Kartikeya to save from Tarakasura, however each time he struck him down with his spear, the torn body parts would once again rejoin. It was then Vishnu who asked him to shatter the Lingam itself, he was wearing as that was the one giving him the invinciblity. However he warned him that those fragments of the Shiva Lingam would once again reunite, so they should be fixed to the ground immediately.

Karthikeya invoked the fiery weapon, Agni-asthra, to shatter the invincible Shiva linga worn by Tarakasura. The intense flames scorched the linga, leaving behind scaly, burn-like markings that devotees can still observe on the five lingas today.

After the lingam split into five fragments, Surya took responsibility for ensuring they would never reunite. He fastened each fragment with nails into the earth. Over each consecrated fragment, temples were built that became the Pancharama shrines.

The Pancharama Kshetras are Amararama( Amaravati, Guntur dt), Draksharama( East Godavari dt), Kumararama( Samarlakota, East Godavari dt), Bhimarama( Bheemavaram, West Godavari dt) and Ksheerarama( Palakollu, West Godavari dt).

Amararamam is situated at Amaravati, in Guntur district, and the reigning deity is Amaralingeswara Swamy. The name comes from the fact that Amarendra installed the Shiva linga here.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!4tVt!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F4d2f467a-5fb0-4e84-bf27-10f81ca1dd2d_3456x2304.jpeg)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!VPAs!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fa80f8431-2686-44a4-bbef-dd63e7275978_480x640.jpeg "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Situated on the **southern bank of the Krishna River, Devi is worshipped as Bala Chamundika here.** The massive Shiva Lingam covering 2 floors, symbolizes **Shiva’s cosmic presence**, stretching beyond ordinary human scale.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!2tTR!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F0ccb820d-d499-41fe-a504-8388229b1a97_3456x2304.jpeg)

![Vasireddy Venkatadri Nayudu - Wikipedia](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!2tTR!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F0ccb820d-d499-41fe-a504-8388229b1a97_3456x2304.jpeg "Vasireddy Venkatadri Nayudu - Wikipedia")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

The temple was developed by Vasireddy Venkatadri Naidu, the ruler of Dharanikota, a prominent zamindar in the late 18th–early 19th century, known for his patronage of temples and culture, and a devotee of Amaralingeswara. Apparently after a massacre of the Chenchu tribe, during a revolt, he sought atonement, and renovating Amararamam was a step towards that.

H not only expanded and beautified the temple, ensuring its grandeur matched its mythic stature, but also appointed nine Archakas, and endowed them with resources for livelihood, embedding social responsibility into his act of penance.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!fj4v!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F62dd2f0f-7699-4f99-af8c-b19528798af7_555x411.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!fj4v!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F62dd2f0f-7699-4f99-af8c-b19528798af7_555x411.png "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!HPIh!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F0a02810c-d0fc-4da4-9763-ebfab76c76ae_680x510.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!HPIh!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F0a02810c-d0fc-4da4-9763-ebfab76c76ae_680x510.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Draksharamam located in East Godavari district, near the town of Ramachandrapuram, is nestled amidst **lush green paddy fields**, that give it a serene, pastoral charm.

Shiva here is worshipped as Bhimeswara Swamy, and his consort Manikyamba Ammavaru is revered as one of the 18 Maha Shakti Peethas. This also makes up the Trilinga Kshetras along with Srisailam and Kaleswaram. As per the Sthala Purana, this was where Sati immolated herself at the infamous Daksha Yagnam, and due to this, no yagnam is conducted to date in the temple.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!XhX6!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F546cabda-273c-4216-a6ca-512e5c3809d4_600x450.jpeg)

![The pond is believed to have the waters of the Sapt Godavari.](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!XhX6!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F546cabda-273c-4216-a6ca-512e5c3809d4_600x450.jpeg "The pond is believed to have the waters of the Sapt Godavari.")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

The Shiva Lingam here is unusually tall, with its lower portion embedded in the basement floor. Only the upper part is visible to devotees, symbolizing Shiva’s cosmic depth, rooted in the unseen, rising into the visible world. Also as per the Sthala Puranam the outer wall could not be completed in time, and remains incomplete even today. It is one of the oldest surviving temples in Andhra Pradesh, dating back to he 10th century CE, built by Bhima, the Eastern Chalukya king of Vengi.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!vV8H!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fcfc6b7b5-2ec2-47e3-9a5a-d9e0c9fd6ed7_2288x1712.jpeg)

![Somarama - Wikipedia](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!vV8H!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fcfc6b7b5-2ec2-47e3-9a5a-d9e0c9fd6ed7_2288x1712.jpeg "Somarama - Wikipedia")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!FczS!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F80c4e02a-3c9d-405e-a5f7-9d1127b40540_434x554.jpeg)

![Somarama Temple – Someswaram Temple – Timings, History](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!FczS!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F80c4e02a-3c9d-405e-a5f7-9d1127b40540_434x554.jpeg "Somarama Temple – Someswaram Temple – Timings, History")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Somaramam, located at Bhimavaram, is the third of the Pancharamas, and the Shiva linga here is believed to be installed by Chandra himself. . It’s believed that Chandra, got rid of his sins by worshipping Shiva here, and hence the name Somaramam. The pond called Chandrakundam is covered with flowers and located at the entrance of the temple.

A unique aspect of the temple is that the Shiva Lingam here changes color as per lunar phases. During Pournami, it turns **white**, symbolizing purity, illumination, and fullness, while during Amavasya, it turns **black**, embodying mystery, introspection, and dissolution.

Also here the **Annapurna temple is built directly above the Shiva temple**,a very rare architectural feature, in a way symbolizing that Shiva’s cosmic energy flows from the sustenance provided by her.

Shakti is revered here as **Sri Rajarajeswari Ammavaru**, embodying regal grace and maternal power. Her presence balances the lunar symbolism with feminine energy, reinforcing the temple’s aura of harmony.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!yHTf!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F4975101e-c7bb-4f40-bc32-c38a85da30eb_1536x2048.jpeg)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!yHTf!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F4975101e-c7bb-4f40-bc32-c38a85da30eb_1536x2048.jpeg "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!EhdA!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F46082bc2-861e-4fa8-bbbd-5eb87ae92dad_800x600.jpeg)

![ప్రధాన గోపురం నుండి దేవాలయ సముదాయ దృశ్యం](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!EhdA!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F46082bc2-861e-4fa8-bbbd-5eb87ae92dad_800x600.jpeg "ప్రధాన గోపురం నుండి దేవాలయ సముదాయ దృశ్యం")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Ksheeraramam at Palakollu in West Godavari is where Lord Shiva is worshipped as Ksheera Ramalingeswara Swamy. As per the Sthala Puranam, **Upamanyu**, son of Kaushika, prayed to Shiva for milk to perform a ritual. Shiva blessed him, and the temple’s **Pushkarni (sacred pond)** overflowed with milk. This gave the name of the place as Palakolanu( pond of milk) which in due course of time became Palakollu.

It is believed that Vishnu consecrated the Shiva Lingam here, and Shakti is worshipped as Parvati Ammavaru, which is **milky white**, symbolizing purity and nourishment.

The temple is around **nine floors high**, making it one of the tallest in Andhra Pradesh. Its **towering gopuram** dominates the landscape and the **mandapa with 72 black pillars** is a marvel of symmetry and craftsmanship. Located on the banks of the **Gosthani**, a tributary of the Godavari River, surrounded by **lush green paddy fields**, the temple offers a soothing presence.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!LN02!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F9a4eb0f0-d737-498c-915f-de993ac09baf_712x617.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!LN02!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F9a4eb0f0-d737-498c-915f-de993ac09baf_712x617.png "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!v7_a!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fd593460a-2e16-453b-8416-15796cc83498_640x475.jpeg)

![The Kumara Rama temple at Samarlakota.](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!v7_a!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fd593460a-2e16-453b-8416-15796cc83498_640x475.jpeg "The Kumara Rama temple at Samarlakota.")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

**Kumara Rama at Samarlakota, East Godavari dt** completes the sacred circuit of the Pancharama Kshetras, and it does so with grandeur and symbolism that ties back to both myth and history. Shiva is worshipped as Kumara Bhimeswara Swamy here, and his consort is Bala Tripura Sundari.

The Shiva Lingam here is believed to have been consecrated by Kartikeya himself, after the slaying of Tarakasura, and the temple was built during the reign of **King Bhima of the Eastern Chalukyas**, hence the name *Kumara Rama*.

Again the Shiva Lingam here is around 16 feet tall, rising to two floors, made entirely of limestone, giving it a distinct texture and aura. The **100-pillar mandapa** is a marvel of symmetry and craftsmanship, while the **Ekasila Nandi** at the entrance, symbolizes devotion and guardianship.

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

1
